questions.stem: A developer wants to validate their ABL agent definition for syntax errors before pushing to the repository. Which CLI command performs offline compilation and validation?
questions.explanation: The 'abl validate' command runs the ABL compiler's parse and validation stages on the specified file(s) without deploying. It catches syntax errors, undefined references, invalid step transitions, and type mismatches. This is ideal for pre-commit hooks and CI/CD pipelines to catch errors early before they reach the platform.

questions.stem: To authenticate the CLI with an Agent Platform instance, you run 'abl ________' which prompts for the platform URL and API key, storing credentials in a local configuration file.
questions.explanation: The 'abl login' command authenticates the CLI with an Agent Platform instance. It prompts for the platform URL and API key, then stores the credentials securely in a local configuration file (~/.abl/config). This enables subsequent commands to interact with the platform without re-entering credentials.

questions.stem: A team uses the CLI in their CI/CD pipeline to automatically deploy agents when code is merged to main. Which command creates a new deployment from agent files in the repository?
questions.explanation: The 'abl deploy' command creates a new deployment in the specified environment. It validates all agent files, creates versioned snapshots, and deploys them to the target environment. The --entry-agent flag specifies which agent receives incoming conversations. Combined with 'abl validate' in a CI pipeline, this ensures only valid agent definitions reach production.

questions.stem: During local development, a developer wants to test their agent interactively from the terminal without opening Studio. Which CLI command starts an interactive chat session?
questions.explanation: The 'abl chat' command starts an interactive conversation with a deployed agent directly from the terminal. It creates a session, sends your typed messages to the agent, and displays responses in real time. This is useful for quick testing during development without switching to the Studio UI.

questions.stem: A developer wants to download all agent definitions from a project to their local repository for version control. Which CLI command exports project agents?
questions.explanation: The 'abl export' command downloads all agent definitions, tool configurations, and project settings from a project to local files. This enables teams to manage agent definitions as code in their version control system. Combined with 'abl deploy', it forms a complete code-as-configuration workflow for agent development.
